title: phaseolus vulgaris var. ceratonoides
intro: Phaseolus vulgaris var. ceratonoides, also known as the creeping bean or wild kidney bean, is a variant of the common bean (Phaseolus vulgaris) that is native to tropical Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Phaseolus vulgaris var. ceratonoides include its potential to reduce the risk of chronic diseases such as heart disease and diabetes, as well as its ability to support digestive health and alleviate symptoms of menopause. Herbal preparations of Phaseolus vulgaris var. ceratonoides can be made by drying and processing the leaves and stems, and can be consumed as teas, infusions, or capsules to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
